methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $2.5B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Varda Space Industries, a Series C-stage space and aerospace company founded by Will Bruey and Delian Asparouhov, is analyzed against a peer universe of 4 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Axiom Space, Impulse Space, Skyryse, Isar Aerospace) and 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL). The target carries a current post-money valuation of $1.5B on $131M in total raised capital.

Two valuation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yielding $2.5B. Peer-median valuation across the 4-company comp set produces $1.5B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $2.5B. Public-comp anchoring is not applicable due to the absence of disclosed revenue data, a common constraint for private companies. Public Space & Aerospace medians trade at 64.2x price-to-sales across the identified tickers, but cannot be applied without target revenue disclosure.

The triangulation of two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $1.3B to $2.9B, with a median of $2.0B. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue and profitability metrics, which would anchor multiples-based approaches, and the limited granularity of stage-progression assumptions across early-stage aerospace companies.
